MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-29-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MERRICK DOWSON'S MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION TO DIFFERENT VENUE, AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT DISCUSSES TRANSFER COSTS WITH COUNSEL. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH AN ADDITION AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT MERRICK DOWSON'S MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION TO DIFFERENT VENUE AND ORDERS THE CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF MADE HER VENUE CHOICE IN GOOD FAITH. IN ADDITION, THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER AS TO THE COST OF TRANSFER NO LATER THAN 1/12/18. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ANDREW Y.S. CHENG CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MITCH LYON, CSR# 6840, MLYON@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED.
TRANSFER ORDER; FEES PAID, TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY FILED PER ORDER BY PLAINTIFF ACKER, DEBORAH

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action To Different Venue, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anthony Giles, Esq. 415-839-2099 for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court discusses transfer costs with Counsel. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with an addition as follows:

DEFENDANT MERRICK DOWSON's Motion To Transfer Action To Different Venue, And For Sanctions.

The Court grants defendant Merrick Dowson's motion to transfer action to different venue and orders the case transferred to San Mateo County. The Court denies defendant's request for sanctions.

Plaintiff alleges mixed causes of action in which most of the alleged injuries occurred outside of the City and County of San Francisco. Pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. 395(a), venue is proper where defendant resides.

The dissolution of marriage proceedings in El Dorado County do not affect the Court's venue analysis. (Boblitt v. Boblitt (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 603, 613.)

Defendant's request for sanctions is denied because the Court finds that plaintiff made her venue choice in good faith.

In addition, the parties shall meet and confer as to the cost of transfer no later than 1/12/18.

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR# 6840, mlyon@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AYSC)
